rules.name: Semantic Color Roles
preconditions: Surface, text, action, border, and status roles are identified.
rules.intent: Assign color to stable semantic roles so hierarchy and state survive theme and viewport changes.
constraints: Name colors by role rather than pigment.
Use accent color only for meaningful emphasis or action.
responsiveBehavior: Keep role meaning unchanged across responsive compositions.
accessibility: Meet contrast requirements and never communicate state through color alone.
antiPatterns: Raw decorative colors without a product or state role.
verification: Inspect computed role usage, contrast, and non-color status cues in every required state.

rules.name: Commerce Trust
preconditions: Price, availability, fulfillment, or supplied proof affects commitment.
rules.intent: Use restrained semantic color to clarify product evidence and commitment state.
constraints: Keep price and fulfillment readable without promotional color noise.
Reserve warning color for actual unavailability or recovery.
responsiveBehavior: Preserve evidence roles when product facts wrap or reorder.
accessibility: Write availability and fulfillment status explicitly alongside color.
antiPatterns: Invented discount colors or fake urgency.
verification: Confirm price, availability, fulfillment, and proof remain distinguishable without color perception.

rules.name: Operational Status
preconditions: Real lifecycle, exception, permission, or connectivity states exist.
rules.intent: Map operational color to real state severity and recovery priority.
constraints: Use a finite documented status palette.
Do not apply severe color to neutral selection or decoration.
responsiveBehavior: Retain text labels when status indicators become compact.
accessibility: Pair every status color with text, iconography, or pattern.
antiPatterns: Rainbow status systems with overlapping meaning.
verification: Review every status mapping for unique meaning, contrast, and a non-color cue.
